county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1860; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Webster County Clerk (Webster Springs). BURNED COUNTY -- 1897 courthouse fire destroyed most records. Formed 1860 from Braxton, Nicholas, and Randolph. Remote, mountainous county. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Check parent counties for pre-1860 records. Webster County Historical Society. Timber industry significant. Substitute sources include state copies of land grants, census records, and tax lists at WV Archives. Holly River State Park area. Webster Springs woodchopping festival documents heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1846; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Ohio River county
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Wetzel County Clerk (New Martinsville). Formed 1846 from Tyler. Ohio River county. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Oil and gas industry. Wetzel County Historical Society. Check Tyler for pre-1846 records. Good surviving records. New Martinsville glass industry (Fenton Art Glass). FamilySearch has microfilmed records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1848; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Wirt County Clerk (Elizabeth). WV's least populous county, formed 1848 from Wood and Jackson. Oil and gas industry -- Burning Springs was an early petroleum site (Civil War target). Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Wirt County Historical Society. Check Wood and Jackson for pre-1848 records. Very small population makes family networks well documented. Good surviving records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1798; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Ohio River county
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Wood County Clerk (Parkersburg). Major Ohio River county formed 1798 from Harrison. Parkersburg was a major B&O Railroad and Ohio River junction. Blennerhassett Island -- Aaron Burr conspiracy records. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Good surviving records. Wood County Historical and Preservation Society. Enslaved population documented. Oil and gas industry. Check Harrison for pre-1798 records. Parkersburg Carnegie Public Library has genealogical collections. Trans-Allegheny Pioneers documented.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1850; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Wyoming County Clerk (Pineville). Formed 1850 from Logan. Major coal county. Coal company records essential -- Pinnickinnick, Clear Fork, and other coal communities. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Wyoming County Historical Society. Check Logan for pre-1850 records. Mine Wars era significant. Twin Falls State Park area. Small pre-coal population. Good surviving records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA
